input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A dark, textured background with faint vertical light streaks transitions as a logo appears. The text 'R.C. Worst co.' is displayed centrally, featuring 'Worst' in a large, blue serif font with white outlines. Below, the slogan 'EXPECT THE BEST FROM R.C. WORST' is written in small black capitals. Soft, circular lens flare artifacts emerge on the left and right sides.</think><answer></answer>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: From 0-4s, an intro sequence displays the 'R.C. Worst co.' logo with the slogan 'EXPECT THE BEST FROM R.C. WORST' against a textured background that transitions to show schematic outlines. Navigation text 'PRODUCTS', 'TRAINING', and 'HOW-TO' fades in below. At 4s, the scene cuts to a showroom where a man with glasses and a black polo with white shoulders stands holding a beige cylinder. To his right, a green pump rests on a blue tank beneath a tall black filter labeled 'INLINE400'. To his left, green and blue tanks sit on a table. From 8s, a blue lower-third graphic appears reading 'Foot Valves Explained With Chris Worst' as the man gestures and holds the object. [14-16] A man in a black and white polo holds a beige cylinder, gesturing right toward a green pump on a blue tank while a blue lower-third graphic displays "Foot Valves Explained With Chris Worst". [16-18] He gestures right, looks down at the cylinder, and places his right hand on top. [18-20] Looking up at the camera with both hands on the object, the blue graphic disappears from the bottom left. [20-22] He shifts his grip to his left hand, looking down at the beige

[50-58] A man in a black and white polo shirt stands beside a green "McDonald" pump assembly mounted on a blue tank and a vertical filter unit labeled "INLINE400". He holds a beige cylindrical object with mesh screening. He looks up from the object to face forward and speak, briefly lifting his right hand to gesture. He then shifts his grip to hold the object with his left hand while gesturing outward with his right, before returning his right hand to hold the cylinder with both hands in front of his chest. A blue "WELL-X-TROL" tank is visible on a table in the background.

From 24-26s, the man separates a cylindrical object into a brass housing and a mesh screen. He holds the mesh screen briefly, then reaches toward the green pump assembly with the brass housing in his right hand, positioning it near the top fitting. Later, from 58-64s, the man in the black and white polo shirt holds the beige cylindrical object with mesh screening using both hands in front of his torso. He looks directly forward at the camera, appearing to speak, before shifting his gaze down to inspect the object. The green pump assembly remains stationary on a blue tank to his right, next to a vertical black filter labeled 'INLINE400'. Finally, from 64-66s, the man turns his torso to the left, dropping his hands to his sides. He shifts his gaze from the cylinder to a green, ribbed container with an open lid situated on the table behind him, inspecting this equipment instead.

[66-74] A man wearing a black and white polo shirt stands beside a green pump assembly mounted on a blue tank, holding a beige cylindrical object with mesh screening in both hands at chest level. To his right, a tall black filter unit labeled "INLINE400" is clearly visible. He maintains a forward-facing posture, initially looking directly at the camera, then shifting his gaze downward to inspect the object closely in his hands, and finally looking back up at the camera as if speaking or explaining. [74-76] He then transitions to holding the brass cylindrical object in his left hand only. He lifts his right hand, keeping the palm open and fingers slightly curled, to gesture near his chest while continuing to face forward. The green pump assembly and the black filter unit remain stationary to his right throughout the sequence.

A man wearing glasses and a black polo with white shoulders stands holding a brass cylinder in his left hand. Throughout the sequence, he gestures with his right hand, moving it from his chest to an open palm, lowering it, and then curling his fingers before opening his palm again. To his right, a green pump assembly rests on a blue tank beside a tall black filter system labeled 'INLINE400' and 'GIANT'. To his left, a blue tank labeled 'WELLXTROL' sits on a green display table. The man maintains eye contact with the camera while speaking.

recent_thinks: [12-14] The man in the black and white polo shirt moves his right hand away from the beige cylinder to gesture towards the green pump assembly on his right. He continues facing forward with the blue lower-third graphic visible in the bottom left corner throughout the sequence.

[22-24] The man in the black and white polo shirt holds the beige cylinder in his left hand while extending his right arm to touch or point at the brass fitting on top of the green pump assembly. [76-78] The man in the black and white polo shirt holds a brass cylindrical object in his left hand. He gestures with his open right hand, moving it from near his chest outwards towards the green pump assembly mounted on the blue tank to his right, while facing forward.

[86-88] A man in a black and white polo shirt holds a brass cylinder in his left hand. He moves his right hand from a low, open position up towards the object, pointing at it while looking down. The green pump assembly on the blue tank remains stationary to his right.

- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
